response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a technically accomplished individual contributor with 15+ years of software development experience and genuine breadth across stacks, but the resume contains no evidence of people management, direct reports, or team leadership beyond contributing to peer-level technical communities. The Engineering Productivity Manager role requires at minimum 2 years of people management and 3 years of technical leadership — neither is demonstrated here. His developer tooling experience (CI setup, test runners, MCP frameworks) is relevant in nature but limited in scale, and there is no exposure to large mobile platform ecosystems. The gap between Staff/Senior IC and a full people-leadership manager role is the central disqualifying factor.
strengths: 15+ years of hands-on software development across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure — well above the 8-year minimum
Genuine developer tooling contributions: CI/CD infrastructure at Altom, custom test runners (Trial, Fluent-Asserts), and MCP code generators at re:cinq
Technical community engagement through workshops, talks, and open-source projects demonstrates knowledge-sharing instincts aligned with an engineering productivity mission
concerns: No demonstrated people management experience — no mention of direct reports, performance reviews, hiring, or team development at any employer
No large-scale mobile platform experience; iOS/Apple TV work at WatchUp was a single-developer maintenance takeover, not platform-level productivity tooling
No organizational change leadership; the role explicitly values 3 years of leading org-level change, and the resume shows no cross-team alignment or strategic roadmap ownership
